title: different behavior from `podman pull` when manifest is 404
body:
/kind bug (Related to RHBZ https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1375323) When trying to pull invalid images from various registries, the behavior of `podman` is not consistent. ``` $ for r in docker.io registry.fedoraproject.org quay.io registry.access.redhat.com registry.centos.org; do sudo podman pull $r/deadbeef; printf "\n"; done Trying to pull docker.io/deadbeef...Failed error pulling image "docker.io/deadbeef": unable to pull docker.io/deadbeef: unable to pull image: Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://deadbeef:latest: Error reading manifest latest in docker.io/library/deadbeef: errors: denied: requested access to the resource is denied unauthorized: authentication required Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/deadbeef...Failed error pulling image "registry.fedoraproject.org/deadbeef": unable to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/deadbeef: unable to pull image: Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://registry.fedoraproject.org/deadbeef:latest: Error reading manifest latest in registry.fedoraproject.org/deadbeef: manifest unknown: manifest unknown Trying to pull quay.io/deadbeef...Failed error pulling image "quay.io/deadbeef": unable to pull quay.io/deadbeef: unable to pull image: Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://quay.io/deadbeef:latest: Error reading manifest latest in quay.io/deadbeef: error parsing HTTP 404 response body: invalid character '<' looking for beginning of value: "\n404 Not Found\n

Not Found

\n

The requested URL was not found on the server. If you entered the URL manually please check your spelling and try again.

\n" Trying to pull registry.access.redhat.com/deadbeef...Failed error pulling image "registry.access.redhat.com/deadbeef": unable to pull registry.access.redhat.com/deadbeef: unable to pull image: Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://registry.access.redhat.com/deadbeef:latest: Error reading manifest latest in registry.access.redhat.com/deadbeef: unknown: Not Found Trying to pull registry.centos.org/deadbeef...Failed error pulling image "registry.centos.org/deadbeef": unable to pull registry.centos.org/deadbeef: unable to pull image: Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://registry.centos.org/deadbeef:latest: Error reading manifest latest in registry.centos.org/deadbeef: manifest unknown: manifest unknown ``` Ultimately, each of the registries returns an HTTP 404 when trying to retrieve the manifest for the non-existent image (well, except for the CentOS registry which returns 403?). ``` $ for r in docker.io registry.fedoraproject.org quay.io registry.access.redhat.com registry.centos.org; do printf "$r\n"; curl -I -L https://$r/v2/deadbeef/manifests/latest; done docker.io HTTP/1.1 301 Moved Permanently Content-length: 0 Location: https://www.docker.com/v2/deadbeef/manifests/latest Connection: close HTTP/2 404 content-type: text/html; charset=UTF-8 content-length: 0 server: nginx date: Thu, 03 Jan 2019 19:46:49 GMT link: ; rel="canonical" link: ; rel="revision" link: ; rel="canonical" link: ; rel="revision" x-ua-compatible: IE=edge content-language: en x-content-type-options: nosniff x-frame-options: SAMEORIGIN last-modified: Thu, 03 Jan 2019 19:23:31 GMT etag: W/"1546543411" content-encoding: gzip x-varnish: 2809607 strict-transport-security: max-age=31536000 age: 262 x-cache: Error from cloudfront via: 1.1 81834769243dfd27a095a32cf5b86723.cloudfront.net (CloudFront) x-amz-cf-id: uwi-OPpwlqSQnrVtwqW5VnJfQ85lUUp1JmZ6y86W9726ytgknQp4CQ== registry.fedoraproject.org HTTP/2 404 date: Thu, 03 Jan 2019 19:51:11 GMT server: Apache/2.4.37 (Fedora) strict-transport-security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload x-frame-options: SAMEORIGIN x-xss-protection: 1; mode=block x-content-type-options: nosniff referrer-policy: same-origin content-type: application/json; charset=utf-8 docker-distribution-api-version: registry/2.0 vary: Accept content-length: 96 x-varnish: 1460070 age: 0 via: 1.1 varnish (Varnish/6.0) apptime: D=282286 appserver: proxy05.fedoraproject.org quay.io HTTP/2 404 server: nginx/1.14.2 date: Thu, 03 Jan 2019 19:51:12 GMT content-type: text/html content-length: 233 registry.access.redhat.com HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found Server: Apache Content-Length: 53 Content-Type: application/json Expires: Thu, 03 Jan 2019 19:51:12 GMT Cache-Control: max-age=0, no-cache, no-store Pragma: no-cache Date: Thu, 03 Jan 2019 19:51:12 GMT Connection: keep-alive X-N: S registry.centos.org HTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden Server: nginx/1.12.2 Date: Thu, 03 Jan 2019 19:51:12 GMT Content-Type: text/html Content-Length: 169 Connection: keep-alive Vary: Accept-Encoding Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000 X-Xss-Protection: 1; mode=block X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff Referrer-Policy: same-origin X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN ``` Is it possible to change the behavior to be consistent when the manifest is 404'ed? These tests were done on Fedora Silverblue 29 with: ``` $ podman --version podman version 0.12.1.2 $ rpm -q podman podman-0.12.1.2-1.git9551f6b.fc29.x86_64 ```

comments.body:
Right, the docker/distribution protocol defines an error reporting mechanism that is not _principally_ based on HTTP status codes, and the “denied+unauthorized”, and “unknown”, and “manifest unknown” are all errors causes that have been returned by the server (and are, to an extent, machine-parseable in Go callers of c/image).

At least in the case of the clearly non-compliant 404+HTML quay.io (unless c/image is somehow to blame for seeing HTML?), it is very reasonable to expect c/image to silently map it to something more useful.

But it’s not _obviously_ correct to map the (reminder, machine-parseable) values “denied”/“unauthorized” into “not found”, or the other way around, nor to just throw away all of them and replace them by a generic “something failed; contact your system administrator :)” — though something like that may well be what a high-level application would decide to do, ignoring the details.

Or maybe, no callers _actually_ care and c/image would be more useful if it represented all of these as a single error type with a single string? We do define an `ErrUnauthorizedForCredentials` in c/image/docker, so it’s not quite a new idea.

From our perspective, I think we'd prefer the more verbose errors - we can always choose to discard them when we print what's gone wrong.

For reference, I believe we originally did that, but switched to our current implementation (print the result of the failed pull) to give more insight into what had gone wrong.
Right, https://github.com/containers/libpod/commit/2188d8f7ada15caddd8b721745a0a4b6f61ce111 replaced a generic string with returning the underlying data.

This is very akin to https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1375323 also filed by @miabbott ... I agree with @mtrmac here.  The more I look at it the more I don't like the idea of trying to map the errors because this really comes from the registry itself ... so the next registryfoobar that comes out, will not match us.
Ok lets close this as close won't fix.
